{"id":"LazyQMK-rgm","title":"Extend docker-entrypoint.sh to fix ALL nested git submodule references","description":"The entrypoint currently only converts /app/qmk_firmware/.git from a submodule pointer to a standalone repo. But QMK has many nested submodules (lib/chibios, lib/chibios-contrib, lib/pico-sdk, lib/printf, lib/vusb, lib/googletest, lib/lvgl, lib/lufa, lib/chibios-contrib/ext/mcux-sdk) that also have .git files pointing to non-existent paths. Use 'find /app/qmk_firmware -name .git -type f' to find ALL .git files and convert each. Must also add safe.directory for each. Then commit the updated entrypoint.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"25952866+Radialarray@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T09:48:58Z","created_by":"Radialarray","updated_at":"2026-02-25T09:48:58Z","labels":["docker","qmk"]}

{"id":"LazyQMK-rg1","title":"Fix key preview not updating after key changes","description":"The keyboard preview doesn't update immediately when a key is changed. The renderMetadata from the backend API takes priority over the local keycodeMap, showing stale data. Fix: When a key changes locally, clear that key's entry from renderMetadata so the reactive keycodeMap fallback is used. Also revert the wrong loadRenderMetadata calls from commit 04a12b7.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"25952866+Radialarray@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T12:31:16Z","created_by":"Radialarray","updated_at":"2026-02-25T12:31:16Z","labels":["bug","frontend"]}

{"id":"LazyQMK-3n9","title":"Add reset button to discard unsaved layout changes","description":"Add a reset/discard button next to the save button in the layout editor. When clicked, it should reload the layout from the backend (last saved state), clearing all local unsaved changes. The button should only be visible/enabled when isDirty is true. Should also reload renderMetadata so rich labels are restored.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"25952866+Radialarray@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T13:39:55Z","created_by":"Radialarray","updated_at":"2026-02-25T13:39:55Z","labels":["frontend"]}

{"id":"LazyQMK-qo5","title":"Wrong combo keys generated for split keyboard layout","description":"When generating QMK firmware for a split keyboard (keebart/corne_choc_pro, LAYOUT_split_3x6_3_ex2), the combo key lookup resolves incorrect keycodes from the layout matrix positions.

**Expected:**
- Combo 2 (Disable RGB): DE_LABK (2,0) + KC_RGHT (6,0)
- Combo 3 (Bootloader): KC_BSPC (3,3) + KC_NO (7,3)

**Actual generated:**
- combo_1_keys[]: {DE_LABK, KC_NO, COMBO_END}  ← right key wrong (KC_NO instead of KC_RGHT)
- combo_2_keys[]: {KC_NO, KC_NO, COMBO_END}    ← both keys wrong (KC_BSPC resolved as KC_NO)
- A phantom COMBO_0 also appears with {LCG(KC_Q), KC_Q} (row 0, cols 0+1 — unrelated keys)

**Root cause hypothesis:**
The combo key resolution likely uses a flat column index that doesn't account for the split gap in the layout table. The right half columns in the .md file (C8-C14) appear to be offset incorrectly, causing the generator to pick up wrong keys or fall back to KC_NO.","acceptance_criteria":"- Combo keys resolve to the correct keycodes for split keyboard layouts
- No phantom combo_0 generated when only combo 2 and combo 3 are defined
- Validated against keebart/corne_choc_pro LAYOUT_split_3x6_3_ex2","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"25952866+Radialarray@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T11:14:00Z","created_by":"Radialarray","updated_at":"2026-03-04T11:14:00Z"}
